stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Just like cities have city flags, some neighborhoods have neighborhood flags.
  • Over forty US neighborhoods have adopted new flags since 2018.
  • The Highland Park neighborhood in Detroit, Michigan, adopted a new flag after residents organized a community design contest.
  • Highland Park’s new flag features local landmarks and colors selected by residents.
  • Residents consider the new flag to be a meaningful symbol of neighborhood identity.
stem: The student wants to make and support a generalization about the effect of adopting a neighborhood flag. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: Over forty US neighborhoods have adopted new flags, including Highland Park, which held a community design contest.
Adopting a neighborhood flag can create a meaningful symbol of neighborhood identity, as was the case when Highland Park adopted a flag featuring local landmarks and resident-selected colors.
Highland Park is just one of over forty US neighborhoods that have adopted new flags.
After residents organized a community design contest, the flag of Highland Park was adopted to feature local landmarks and resident-selected colors.

rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The sentence makes and supports a generalization about the effect of adopting a neighborhood flag, noting that adopting one can create a meaningful symbol of neighborhood identity, as was the case in Highland Park.

Choice A is incorrect because the sentence explains that many US neighborhoods have adopted flags and provides an example; it doesn’t make and support a generalization about the effect. Choice C is incorrect because the sentence provides an example without making a generalization. Choice D is incorrect because the sentence emphasizes how the flag of Highland Park was adopted; it doesn’t make and support a generalization about the effect.
